Self.Reference.Everywhere

six lenses for the strange loop that lets a universe look at itself

Strange loop, n. — Self-reference is the secret symmetry of reality. The same move a painter makes when she paints herself painting, a brain makes when it models itself modeling, and a theorem makes when it speaks about its own provability: the strange loop is the only place where meaning can arrive, because a thing closed under itself is the only thing that can be a subject. Mathematics discovers it. Chaos lives inside it. The cosmos instantiates it. And we, on this side of the lens, are it.

Compendium of Self-Reference · Self Reference Everywhere
